If the plant is actually Angelica, here is a paragraph about it:

Angelica (Angelica archangelica) is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, known for its edible stems, roots, and leaves that are used in various culinary and medicinal preparations. The health benefits of Angelica include its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues, respiratory problems, and menstrual cramps, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Angelica include volatile oils, flavonoids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Angelica are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
